| Version | Date       | Changes         |
| ------- | ---------- | --------------- |
| v1      | 2026-01-17 | Initial release |
| v1.1    | 2026-04-22 | Added Applicability Tier Table per POL-GOV-001 §8.8. |
| v1.2    | 2026-04-22 | **RFC-0002 cross-link (additive).** New §11 AI-Assisted Code & Prompt Injection — secure-coding-side checkpoints for AI-assisted PRs (license contamination, hallucinated APIs, plausible-but-wrong crypto) and prompt-injection defenses (structural separation, input validation before prompt composition, output sanitization when LLM output reaches an interpreter, tool-call authorization, retrieval grounding). Cross-refs [48] STD-AI-001 §2 for the engineering-practice home. No existing clauses changed; no T1/T2/T3 tier migrations. |



---

## Quick Reference Card — STD-SEC-002 Secure Coding Directive

**Authority**: CYBERCUBE Secure Coding Standard v1 | Owner: Security Team | Effective: 2026-01-17

**Scope**: All CYBERCUBE application source code · All languages, frameworks, runtimes · Excludes: AuthN, AuthZ, Crypto (covered by dedicated standards)

### Design Principles (MANDATORY)

- Secure by Default
- Defense in Depth
- Least Privilege
- Fail Securely
- Trust No Input
- Simplicity over complexity

### Core Control Domains

**Input Validation**: Validate ALL external input · Allowlist > blocklist · Schema-based validation at trust boundaries

**Output Encoding**: Context-specific encoding required · Framework auto-escaping preferred · Raw HTML injection prohibited

**Injection Prevention**: Parameterized queries ONLY · No string concatenation in queries

**File & Path Safety**: Magic-byte validation · Canonicalized paths · Upload directory confinement

**Dependency Security**: Lock files mandatory · Automated vulnerability scanning · Known-vulnerable versions prohibited

**Secrets Handling**: Env vars / secrets manager only · No secrets in code, logs, or URLs

**Error Handling**: Generic user-facing errors · Detailed internal logs only · No stack traces exposed

**Authorization (Code-Level)**: Check on EVERY operation · No fetch-then-filter · Same response for 403 / 404

**Testing & Verification**: Security tests mandatory · IDOR, traversal, injection tests required

### Governance

- OWASP Top-10 aligned
- Enforced via CI gates
- Non-compliance = audit finding

**Status**: ACTIVE / ENFORCED

---

## Scorable Compliance Matrix — Secure Coding (S1–S10)

**Dimensions**: S1–S10 | **Scale**: 0–5 | **Max**: 50 points (10 × 5)

### Scoring Scale

| Score | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| 0 | Not defined / unmanaged |
| 1 | Defined only |
| 2 | Partially implemented |
| 3 | Implemented (baseline compliant) |
| 4 | Enforced, measured |
| 5 | Institutionalized, audited |

### Dimensions

| ID | Dimension | Criteria | 0–1 | 4–5 |
|----|-----------|----------|-----|-----|
| S1 | Security Governance & Ownership | Standard approved/owned · Applicability defined · SDLC gate enforcement | No formal secure coding authority | Standard enforced, owned, audited |
| S2 | Access Control (Code-Level) | AuthZ per operation · No fetch-then-filter · Privileged paths guarded | Inconsistent/implicit access checks | Least-privilege enforced and tested |
| S3 | Data Handling & Input Validation | All external input validated · Allowlist-based · Trust boundaries defined | Input trusted by default | Systematic validation with tests |
| S4 | Output Encoding & Injection Defense | Context-aware encoding · Parameterized queries · No raw concat | Injection risks unmanaged | Injection classes eliminated |
| S5 | Application Security Practices | Secure-by-default · Dependency governed · Unsafe APIs prohibited | Security left to developers | Security embedded in standards |
| S6 | Error Handling & Logging | Fail-secure · No sensitive data in errors/logs · Forensic-ready logs | Verbose/unsafe error output | Controlled, security-aware logging |
| S7 | Security Testing & Verification | Mandatory test cases · Common attack coverage · Findings tracked | No security testing | Automated, repeatable, enforced |
| S8 | Dependency & Supply Chain Security | Version pinning · Vuln scanning · Known-vulnerable deps blocked | Blind dependency usage | Continuous dependency governance |
| S9 | Developer Training & Awareness | Secure coding training · OWASP-aligned · Dev-time guidance | No secure coding education | Training measured and reinforced |
| S10 | Compliance & Auditability | Controls traceable · Evidence on demand · CI/CD artifacts | Non-auditable secure coding | Audit-ready with objective evidence |

### Thresholds

| Range | Rating |
|-------|--------|
| >=45 | Secure Coding Maturity |
| 36–44 | Managed with minor gaps |
| 28–35 | Elevated application risk |
| <28 | Unacceptable security posture |

### Hard Fail Conditions

- **S1 < 3** → No enforceable standard
- **S3 < 3** → Unsafe input handling
- **S7 < 3** → No security verification


# CYBERCUBE Vulnerability Management Standard (v1.1)

**Standard ID:** STD-SEC-006
**Status:** Active
**Effective:** 2026-01-17 (v1), 2026-04-22 (v1.1)
**Classification:** INTERNAL
**Owner:** Security Team
**Applies to:** All CYBERCUBE systems, applications, and infrastructure

## Applicability Tier Table

| Applicability | Tier | Summary of Clauses in This Standard | Waiver Path |
| ------------- | ---- | ----------------------------------- | ----------- |
| All projects | **T1 MUST** | (1) Dependency scanning MUST run on every build in CI (Dependabot/Renovate/equivalent). (2) Critical and High vulnerabilities in production-path dependencies MUST be triaged within 5 business days. (3) Known-exploited (CISA KEV) vulnerabilities MUST be remediated within SLA (Critical ≤ 7d, High ≤ 30d, Medium ≤ 90d). (4) A vulnerability intake channel (email alias or ticket form) MUST exist and be documented in the project README. (5) No production deploy MAY proceed with known Critical vulnerabilities in direct runtime dependencies without a documented waiver per STD-GOV-003. | None (non-waivable for clauses 1–5; clause 5 itself is the waiver path for rare exceptions) |
| SaaS / customer-facing | **T2 SHOULD** | SAST on every PR with blocking gate, container image scanning pre-push, vulnerability tracking tool (not just spreadsheet), published Vulnerability Disclosure Policy (VDP / security.txt), triage workflow with defined severity matrix, monthly exposure metrics. | Lightweight waiver per POL-GOV-001 §8.3 |
| Regulated / high-risk | **T3 MAY** | Bug bounty program, external researcher portal, DAST in staging, cloud CSPM (Config, Inspector, Defender), executive reporting on vulnerability KRIs (STD-GOV-005), third-party penetration tests on scheduled cadence, metrics dashboard wired to UCM evidence pipeline. | Formal waiver per STD-GOV-003 |

> Per POL-GOV-001 §8.8.

> **v1.1 (2026-04-22) — Unfreeze (Path B).** T1 reduced to five enforceable rules: dep-scan-in-CI, triage SLA, KEV remediation SLA, intake channel, release gate on Critical. VDP, bug bounty, researcher portal reclassified to T2/T3 ROADMAP. CVSS 3.1 and SLA definitions remain normative as T1 supporting text.

## 0. Purpose & Design Principles

This standard defines how CYBERCUBE identifies, assesses, prioritizes, and
remediates security vulnerabilities across all systems. It establishes
severity classification, remediation SLAs, responsible disclosure procedures,
and vulnerability reward program guidelines.

Industry alignment:

- NIST SP 800-40 (Patch Management)
- ISO/IEC 27001 (A.12.6 Technical Vulnerability Management)
- PCI DSS Requirement 6 (Secure Systems)
- SOC 2 Type II (CC7.1)
- CISA Binding Operational Directives
- FIRST CVSS Standards

Design principles:

1. **Risk-Based** — Prioritize by actual risk, not just severity
2. **Timely** — Remediate within defined SLAs
3. **Comprehensive** — Cover all assets and dependencies
4. **Continuous** — Ongoing assessment, not point-in-time
5. **Collaborative** — Work with researchers and vendors
6. **Transparent** — Clear disclosure and communication

This document does NOT define:

- Security incident response — see Incident Response Standard
- Secure development — see Testing & Quality Assurance Standard
- Change management — see Release & Deployment Standard

## 1. Vulnerability Sources

Vulnerabilities are identified through multiple channels.

### 1.1 Internal Sources

| Source                    | Frequency              | Coverage              |
| ------------------------- | ---------------------- | --------------------- |
| SAST (Static Analysis)    | Every commit           | Application code      |
| DAST (Dynamic Analysis)   | Weekly                 | Running applications  |
| SCA (Dependency Scanning) | Daily                  | Third-party libraries |
| Container Scanning        | Every build            | Container images      |
| Infrastructure Scanning   | Weekly                 | Servers, networks     |
| Cloud Configuration       | Continuous             | Cloud resources       |
| Penetration Testing       | Annual + major changes | Full scope            |
| Code Review               | Every PR               | Application code      |

### 1.2 External Sources

| Source                 | Monitoring  | Response             |
| ---------------------- | ----------- | -------------------- |
| NVD/CVE                | Continuous  | Automated matching   |
| CISA KEV               | Continuous  | Priority remediation |
| Vendor Advisories      | Continuous  | Vendor-specific      |
| Threat Intelligence    | Continuous  | Proactive assessment |
| Bug Bounty Reports     | As received | Per disclosure SLA   |
| Responsible Disclosure | As received | Per disclosure SLA   |
| Security Researchers   | As received | Per disclosure SLA   |
| Customer Reports       | As received | Triage immediately   |

### 1.3 Vulnerability Intake

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      VULNERABILITY INTAKE FLOW                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  INTERNAL SOURCES              EXTERNAL SOURCES                            │
│  ┌──────────────┐             ┌──────────────┐                            │
│  │ SAST/DAST    │             │ NVD/CVE      │                            │
│  │ SCA          │             │ CISA KEV     │                            │
│  │ Pen Tests    │             │ Vendor       │                            │
│  │ Scanning     │             │ Researchers  │                            │
│  └──────┬───────┘             └──────┬───────┘                            │
│         │                            │                                      │
│         └──────────┬─────────────────┘                                      │
│                    │                                                        │
│                    ▼                                                        │
│         ┌──────────────────┐                                               │
│         │ VULNERABILITY    │                                               │
│         │ MANAGEMENT       │                                               │
│         │ SYSTEM           │                                               │
│         └────────┬─────────┘                                               │
│                  │                                                          │
│         ┌────────┴────────┐                                                │
│         ▼                 ▼                                                │
│  ┌─────────────┐   ┌─────────────┐                                        │
│  │ AUTOMATED   │   │ MANUAL      │                                        │
│  │ TRIAGE      │   │ TRIAGE      │                                        │
│  └─────────────┘   └─────────────┘                                        │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.4 Scanning Integration

Scanner findings flow into the vulnerability management system automatically
via CI/CD pipeline integration:

| Scanner Type   | Integration Point                   | Ticket Creation                          |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| SAST           | PR check / commit hook              | Auto-create on High+                     |
| SCA            | `npm audit` / `pip-audit` in CI | Auto-create on High+                     |
| DAST           | Scheduled pipeline stage            | Auto-create on Medium+                   |
| Container      | Image build step                    | Block build on Critical; ticket on High+ |
| Infrastructure | Scheduled scan agent                | Auto-create on Medium+                   |

**Requirements:**

- All scanners report to a central vulnerability management system
- Deduplication logic prevents duplicate tickets for the same CVE/finding
- Findings below auto-create threshold are batched into weekly triage review
- Scanner-to-ticket mapping is auditable (finding ID → ticket ID)

---

## 2. Severity Classification

Vulnerabilities are classified using CVSS with contextual adjustments.

### 2.1 CVSS Scoring

CYBERCUBE uses CVSS 3.1 as the baseline severity score. CVSS 4.0 was released
by FIRST in November 2023 and is available for adoption; migration is planned
for a future version of this standard.

| CVSS Score | Severity      | Color  |
| ---------- | ------------- | ------ |
| 9.0 - 10.0 | Critical      | Red    |
| 7.0 - 8.9  | High          | Orange |
| 4.0 - 6.9  | Medium        | Yellow |
| 0.1 - 3.9  | Low           | Blue   |
| 0.0        | Informational | Gray   |

### 2.2 CVSS Vector Components

```
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H

AV = Attack Vector (Network, Adjacent, Local, Physical)
AC = Attack Complexity (Low, High)
PR = Privileges Required (None, Low, High)
UI = User Interaction (None, Required)
S  = Scope (Unchanged, Changed)
C  = Confidentiality Impact (None, Low, High)
I  = Integrity Impact (None, Low, High)
A  = Availability Impact (None, Low, High)
```

### 2.3 Contextual Severity Adjustment

Base CVSS scores are adjusted based on context.

**Stacking rule:** Apply at most one upward adjustment and one downward
adjustment. When multiple upward factors apply, use the Effective Severity
Matrix (§2.4) as the authoritative lookup. Active exploitation always
overrides to Critical regardless of other factors.

| Factor                      | Adjustment        |
| --------------------------- | ----------------- |
| Internet-exposed            | +1 severity level |
| Customer data at risk       | +1 severity level |
| Active exploitation (KEV)   | → Critical       |
| Public exploit available    | +1 severity level |
| Compensating controls exist | -1 severity level |
| Internal-only system        | -1 severity level |
| No sensitive data           | -1 severity level |

### 2.4 Effective Severity Matrix

| Base CVSS | Internet Exposed | Customer Data | Internal Only |
| --------- | ---------------- | ------------- | ------------- |
| Critical  | Critical         | Critical      | High          |
| High      | Critical         | Critical      | High          |
| Medium    | High             | High          | Medium        |
| Low       | Medium           | Medium        | Low           |

### 2.5 Auto-Critical Triggers

The following automatically escalate to Critical:

- CISA Known Exploited Vulnerability (KEV)
- Active exploitation confirmed
- CVSS 9.0+ on internet-exposed system
- Authentication bypass on production
- Remote code execution on production
- SQL injection with data access
- Confirmed or probable customer PII exposure (based on data flow analysis)

## 3. Vulnerability Triage

All vulnerabilities undergo triage to validate and prioritize.

### 3.1 Triage Process

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        VULNERABILITY TRIAGE                                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  1. VALIDATE           2. CLASSIFY           3. PRIORITIZE                 │
│  ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐               │
│  │ Confirm real │────▶│ Assign CVSS  │────▶│ Apply context│               │
│  │ False pos?   │     │ Categorize   │     │ Rank order   │               │
│  │ Duplicate?   │     │ Affected     │     │ Assign owner │               │
│  └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────────┘               │
│         │                                         │                        │
│    ┌────┴────┐                                   │                        │
│    │         │                                   ▼                        │
│   Valid   Invalid                         ┌──────────────┐               │
│    │      (close)                         │ ASSIGN       │               │
│    ▼                                      │ REMEDIATION  │               │
│  Continue                                 │ DEADLINE     │               │
│                                           └──────────────┘               │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.2 Triage SLAs

| Severity | Triage Completion |
| -------- | ----------------- |
| Critical | 4 hours           |
| High     | 24 hours          |
| Medium   | 3 business days   |
| Low      | 5 business days   |

### 3.3 Triage Checklist

```markdown
## Vulnerability Triage Checklist

### Validation
- [ ] Vulnerability confirmed (not false positive)
- [ ] Not a duplicate of existing finding
- [ ] Affected systems identified
- [ ] Exploitability verified

### Classification
- [ ] CVSS score assigned
- [ ] Base severity determined
- [ ] Context adjustments applied
- [ ] Effective severity set

### Prioritization
- [ ] Asset criticality considered
- [ ] Exposure assessed
- [ ] Active exploitation checked (CISA KEV)
- [ ] Compensating controls evaluated

### Assignment
- [ ] Owner assigned
- [ ] Remediation deadline set
- [ ] Ticket created
- [ ] Stakeholders notified
```

### 3.4 Vulnerability Ticket Template

```yaml
# Vulnerability Ticket

id: VULN-2026-001
title: "SQL Injection in User Search API"
status: open

# Classification
cve: CVE-2026-12345
cvss_score: 8.6
cvss_vector: "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:L"
base_severity: high
effective_severity: critical
adjustment_reason: "Internet-exposed, customer data at risk"

# Impact
affected_systems:
  - api-service (production)
  - api-service (staging)
affected_component: "src/api/users/search.ts"
data_at_risk: "Customer PII"

# Risk Factors
internet_exposed: true
active_exploitation: false
public_exploit: true
compensating_controls: "WAF rule deployed"

# Assignment
owner: "@alice"
team: "api-team"
created: "2026-01-17T10:00:00Z"
due_date: "2026-01-18T10:00:00Z"
sla_hours: 24

# Resolution
resolution_type: null  # patch, config, workaround, risk_accept
resolved_at: null
resolution_notes: null
```

## 4. Remediation SLAs

Vulnerabilities must be remediated within defined timeframes.

### 4.1 Standard Remediation SLAs

| Effective Severity | Remediation SLA | Grace Period |
| ------------------ | --------------- | ------------ |
| Critical           | 24 hours        | None         |
| High               | 7 days          | 3 days       |
| Medium             | 30 days         | 7 days       |
| Low                | 90 days         | 30 days      |
| Informational      | Best effort     | N/A          |

### 4.2 Expedited SLAs (CISA KEV / Active Exploitation)

| Condition                     | Remediation SLA                                |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| CISA KEV (internet-facing)    | 24 hours                                       |
| CISA KEV (internal)           | 7 days                                         |
| Confirmed active exploitation | 24 hours                                       |
| Public exploit available      | Reduce SLA by 50% (minimum: High SLA / 7 days) |

### 4.3 SLA Calculation

```typescript
interface VulnerabilitySLA {
  severity: 'critical' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low';
  isKEV: boolean;
  isExploited: boolean;
  hasPublicExploit: boolean;
  isInternetFacing: boolean;
}

function calculateSLAHours(vuln: VulnerabilitySLA): number {
  // Base SLAs
  const baseSLA = {
    critical: 24,
    high: 168,      // 7 days
    medium: 720,    // 30 days
    low: 2160,      // 90 days
  };
  
  let slaHours = baseSLA[vuln.severity];
  
  // Active exploitation → always 24 hours
  if (vuln.isExploited) {
    return 24;
  }
  
  // CISA KEV → 24 hours if internet-facing, 7 days if internal
  if (vuln.isKEV) {
    return vuln.isInternetFacing ? 24 : 168;
  }
  
  // Public exploit → reduce by 50%, but never below High SLA (7 days)
  if (vuln.hasPublicExploit) {
    slaHours = Math.max(Math.floor(slaHours / 2), 168);
  }
  
  return slaHours;
}
```

### 4.4 SLA Extensions

Extensions require approval:

| Extension Request | Approver              | Maximum Extension |
| ----------------- | --------------------- | ----------------- |
| Critical          | CISO                  | 24 hours          |
| High              | Security Lead         | 7 days            |
| Medium            | Security Team         | 14 days           |
| Low               | Vulnerability Manager | 30 days           |

Extension requirements:

- Compensating controls documented
- Business justification provided
- Risk acceptance signed (if > 2x SLA)
- Follow-up date scheduled

### 4.5 SLA Exceptions

Some vulnerabilities may qualify for exceptions:

| Exception Type    | Criteria                  | Process                       |
| ----------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Vendor dependency | Patch not available       | Document, monitor, compensate |
| System retirement | < 30 days to decommission | Approve, isolate, monitor     |
| Major refactor    | Fix requires redesign     | Approve, compensate, track    |
| False positive    | Confirmed not exploitable | Document, close               |

## 5. Remediation Process

Vulnerabilities are fixed through a controlled process.

### 5.1 Remediation Options

| Option               | Description                     | Preference  |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------- | ----------- |
| Patch                | Apply vendor security update    | Preferred   |
| Upgrade              | Update to fixed version         | Preferred   |
| Configuration        | Change settings to mitigate     | Acceptable  |
| Code Fix             | Custom fix for application code | Acceptable  |
| Compensating Control | Alternative control             | Temporary   |
| Risk Acceptance      | Accept residual risk            | Last resort |

### 5.2 Remediation Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      REMEDIATION WORKFLOW                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  │
│  │ IDENTIFY FIX │─▶│   DEVELOP    │─▶│    TEST      │─▶│   DEPLOY     │  │
│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  │
│        │                 │                 │                 │            │
│        ▼                 ▼                 ▼                 ▼            │
│    Patch, config,    Implement or     Verify fix,      Deploy via        │
│    code change       integrate        regression       standard          │
│                                                        pipeline          │
│                                                              │            │
│                                           ┌──────────────────┘            │
│                                           ▼                              │
│                                    ┌──────────────┐                      │
│                                    │   VERIFY     │                      │
│                                    └──────────────┘                      │
│                                           │                              │
│                                           ▼                              │
│                                    Re-scan, confirm                      │
│                                    no longer vulnerable                  │
│                                           │                              │
│                                           ▼                              │
│                                    ┌──────────────┐                      │
│                                    │    CLOSE     │                      │
│                                    └──────────────┘                      │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 5.3 Emergency Patching

For Critical vulnerabilities with active exploitation:

```markdown
## Emergency Patch Process

### Criteria
- Critical severity
- Active exploitation OR
- CISA KEV with short deadline OR
- Imminent customer risk

### Expedited Process
1. Security declares emergency
2. Notify on-call engineering
3. Develop/obtain fix
4. Abbreviated testing (security + smoke)
5. Deploy immediately
6. Enhanced monitoring
7. Full regression next business day

### Approvals
- Security Lead: Declare emergency
- Engineering Lead: Approve expedited deployment
- On-Call: Execute deployment

### Documentation
- Document emergency rationale
- Post-deployment review within 48 hours
```

### 5.4 Compensating Controls

When immediate remediation isn't possible:

| Control Type | Examples                     | Duration      |
| ------------ | ---------------------------- | ------------- |
| Network      | Firewall rule, segmentation  | Until patched |
| Application  | WAF rule, input validation   | Until patched |
| Monitoring   | Enhanced logging, alerting   | Until patched |
| Access       | Restrict access, require MFA | Until patched |
| Process      | Manual review, approval      | Until patched |

Compensating control requirements:

- [ ] Documented in vulnerability ticket
- [ ] Approved by Security
- [ ] Implemented and verified
- [ ] Monitored for effectiveness
- [ ] Removed after remediation

### 5.5 Risk Acceptance

Risk acceptance is a last resort:

```markdown
## Risk Acceptance Request

### Vulnerability Information
- ID: VULN-2026-001
- CVE: CVE-2026-12345
- Severity: High
- Affected System: legacy-app

### Justification
[Why remediation is not feasible]

### Risk Assessment
- Likelihood: [High/Medium/Low]
- Impact: [High/Medium/Low]
- Residual Risk: [High/Medium/Low]

### Compensating Controls
1. [Control 1]
2. [Control 2]

### Acceptance Terms
- Review Date: [Date, max 90 days]
- Conditions: [Any conditions that would void acceptance]

### Approvals
- [ ] Asset Owner: [Name, Date]
- [ ] Security Lead: [Name, Date]
- [ ] CISO (if Critical/High): [Name, Date]
- [ ] Executive (if Critical): [Name, Date]
```

## 6. Responsible Disclosure

CYBERCUBE welcomes security research and responsible disclosure.

### 6.1 Vulnerability Disclosure Policy (VDP)

```markdown
# CYBERCUBE Vulnerability Disclosure Policy

## Scope
We welcome reports of security vulnerabilities in:
- cybercube.software and subdomains
- CYBERCUBE applications and APIs
- CYBERCUBE mobile applications

## Out of Scope
- Third-party services
- Social engineering attacks
- Physical security
- Denial of service testing
- Spam or phishing

## How to Report
Email: security@cybercube.software
PGP Key: [key fingerprint]

Include:
- Description of the vulnerability
- Steps to reproduce
- Potential impact
- Your contact information

## Our Commitment
- Acknowledge within 2 business days
- Initial assessment within 5 business days
- Keep you informed of remediation progress
- Credit you (if desired) after fix
- Not pursue legal action for good-faith research

## Your Commitment
- Do not access data beyond what's necessary
- Do not modify or delete data
- Do not disrupt services
- Allow reasonable time for remediation
- Do not disclose publicly before coordinated disclosure

## Safe Harbor
We will not pursue legal action against researchers who:
- Act in good faith
- Follow this policy
- Report vulnerabilities promptly
- Do not exploit vulnerabilities maliciously
```

### 6.2 Disclosure Timeline

| Event                   | Timeline                     |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| Report received         | Day 0                        |
| Acknowledgment          | Day 0-2                      |
| Initial triage          | Day 5                        |
| Remediation plan shared | Day 14                       |
| Remediation complete    | Per SLA                      |
| Coordinated disclosure  | Day 90 (max) or after fix    |
| Public disclosure       | After coordinated disclosure |

### 6.3 Disclosure Response Process

```
Report Received
      │
      ▼
  Acknowledge (2 days)
      │
      ▼
  Triage & Validate (5 days)
      │
      ├──▶ Invalid → Respond with explanation
      │
      ▼
  Valid → Create internal ticket
      │
      ▼
  Assess severity & SLA
      │
      ▼
  Communicate plan to reporter (14 days)
      │
      ▼
  Remediate (per SLA)
      │
      ▼
  Verify fix
      │
      ▼
  Coordinate disclosure with reporter
      │
      ▼
  Publish advisory (if warranted)
      │
      ▼
  Credit researcher (if desired)
```

### 6.4 Researcher Communication Template

```markdown
## Initial Acknowledgment

Subject: Re: Security Vulnerability Report - [Reference ID]

Dear [Researcher],

Thank you for reporting a potential security vulnerability to CYBERCUBE.
We take security seriously and appreciate your responsible disclosure.

Your report has been assigned reference ID: [VDP-2026-001]

Our security team will review your submission and provide an initial
assessment within 5 business days. We will keep you informed of our
progress throughout the remediation process.

If you have any questions, please reply to this email.

Best regards,
CYBERCUBE Security Team


## Status Update

Subject: Security Report [VDP-2026-001] - Status Update

Dear [Researcher],

We have completed our assessment of your reported vulnerability.

Status: [Confirmed/Not Applicable/Duplicate]
Severity: [Critical/High/Medium/Low]
Expected Remediation: [Date]

[Additional details as appropriate]

We will notify you when the fix has been deployed.

Thank you for helping us improve our security.

Best regards,
CYBERCUBE Security Team


## Resolution Notification

Subject: Security Report [VDP-2026-001] - Resolved

Dear [Researcher],

We are pleased to inform you that the vulnerability you reported has
been remediated.

- Issue: [Brief description]
- Resolution: [How it was fixed]
- Deployed: [Date]

We would like to publicly acknowledge your contribution to our security.
Please let us know:
- How you would like to be credited (name, handle, company)
- Whether you consent to public acknowledgment

Thank you for your responsible disclosure and patience.

Best regards,
CYBERCUBE Security Team
```

## 7. Bug Bounty Program

CYBERCUBE operates a vulnerability reward program for qualifying findings.

### 7.1 Program Overview

```markdown
# CYBERCUBE Bug Bounty Program

## Eligibility
- Open to all security researchers
- Must follow Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
- Must not be a current employee or contractor
- Must not be a resident of sanctioned countries

## Reward Amounts

| Severity | Reward Range |
|----------|--------------|
| Critical | $2,000 - $10,000 |
| High | $500 - $2,000 |
| Medium | $100 - $500 |
| Low | $50 - $100 |

Exact amounts determined by:
- Vulnerability impact
- Report quality
- Exploitability
- Novelty

## Qualifying Vulnerabilities
- Remote code execution
- SQL injection
- Authentication bypass
- Authorization flaws
- Sensitive data exposure
- Cross-site scripting (stored/reflected)
- Server-side request forgery
- Insecure direct object references

## Non-Qualifying Issues
- Denial of service
- Social engineering
- Physical attacks
- Spam/phishing
- Self-XSS
- Missing best practices without impact
- Issues in out-of-scope systems
- Previously reported issues
```

### 7.2 Reward Determination Matrix

| Impact \ Exploitability             | Easy             | Moderate | Difficult |
| ----------------------------------- | ---------------- | -------- | --------- |
| Critical (RCE, SQLi, Auth bypass)   | $10,000 | $5,000 | $2,000   |           |
| High (Data exposure, Priv esc)      | $2,000 | $1,000  | $500     |           |
| Medium (XSS, CSRF, Info disclosure) | $500 | $250      | $100     |           |
| Low (Minor info leak)               | $100 | $75       | $50      |           |

### 7.3 Bug Bounty Process

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      BUG BOUNTY PROCESS                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  1. SUBMIT           2. TRIAGE            3. ASSESS                        │
│  ┌──────────────┐   ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐                  │
│  │ Researcher   │──▶│ Validate     │───▶│ Determine    │                  │
│  │ reports via  │   │ & classify   │    │ severity &   │                  │
│  │ bounty portal│   │              │    │ reward       │                  │
│  └──────────────┘   └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘                  │
│                            │                    │                          │
│                       ┌────┴────┐              │                          │
│                       │         │              │                          │
│                    Valid    Invalid            ▼                          │
│                       │       (N/A)     ┌──────────────┐                  │
│                       ▼                 │ 4. REMEDIATE │                  │
│                    Continue             └──────────────┘                  │
│                                                │                          │
│  5. VERIFY           6. PAY              7. PUBLISH                       │
│  ┌──────────────┐   ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐                  │
│  │ Confirm fix  │──▶│ Process      │───▶│ Credit       │                  │
│  │              │   │ payment      │    │ researcher   │                  │
│  └──────────────┘   └──────────────┘    │ (opt-in)     │                  │
│                                         └──────────────┘                  │
│                                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 8. Metrics & Reporting

Vulnerability management effectiveness is measured and reported.

### 8.1 Key Metrics

| Metric                | Description                    | Target     |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------ | ---------- |
| MTTD                  | Mean time to detect (internal) | < 7 days   |
| MTTR                  | Mean time to remediate         | Within SLA |
| SLA Compliance        | % remediated within SLA        | > 95%      |
| Open Critical         | Count of open Critical vulns   | 0          |
| Open High             | Count of open High vulns       | < 10       |
| Vulnerability Density | Vulns per 1000 LOC             | Decreasing |
| Recurrence Rate       | Same vuln type recurrence      | < 10%      |
| False Positive Rate   | Invalid findings               | < 10%      |
| Risk Acceptance Count | Open risk acceptances          | < 5        |

### 8.2 Reporting Cadence

| Report                  | Frequency | Audience          |
| ----------------------- | --------- | ----------------- |
| Vulnerability Dashboard | Real-time | Security team     |
| Weekly Summary          | Weekly    | Engineering leads |
| Monthly Report          | Monthly   | Leadership        |
| Quarterly Review        | Quarterly | Executives        |
| Annual Assessment       | Annually  | Board/Audit       |

```markdown
| Low | 67 | 12 | 89 |

- Critical: 100% (2/2)
- High: 93% (14/15)
- Medium: 89% (40/45)
- Low: 85% (76/89)

- Closed this month: 151
```

Print it. Keep it handy.

### Severity Levels (CVSS)

| Score    | Severity |
| -------- | -------- |
| 9.0-10.0 | Critical |
| 7.0-8.9  | High     |
| 4.0-6.9  | Medium   |
| 0.1-3.9  | Low      |

### Remediation SLAs

| Severity | SLA      |
| -------- | -------- |
| Critical | 24 hours |
| High     | 7 days   |
| Medium   | 30 days  |
| Low      | 90 days  |

### Auto-Critical Triggers

- CISA KEV
- Active exploitation
- CVSS 9.0+ internet-facing
- Auth bypass (prod)
- RCE (prod)
- SQLi with data access

### Triage SLAs

| Severity | Triage   |
| -------- | -------- |
| Critical | 4 hours  |
| High     | 24 hours |
| Medium   | 3 days   |
| Low      | 5 days   |

```
Report → Ack (2d) → Triage (5d) → 
Plan (14d) → Fix (SLA) → Disclose (90d max)
```
